path: 01 Journal/2025/2025-10 October/Resources/2025-10-02 The Asset Administration Shell as Enabler for MX Port Leo___IDTA.pdf
text: The Asset Administration Shell as Enabler for MX Port Leo Andreas Orzelski (Phoenix Contact) Michael Hoffmeister (Hochschule Karlsruhe) Sandeep Rudra (IDTA) IDTA Open Tech days 2025 MX-Port Leo: Overview Immediate entry possible for data providers and data consumers ▪ A data provider only needs to provide an AAS server in accordance with IDTA / IEC 63278 ▪ No further requirements need to be met to provide data ▪ Data consumer can access data in accordance with IDTA / IEC 63278 Operation possible without 3rd party operating company ▪ Neither a data provider nor a data consumer needs to register at any 3rd party Each company operates its own system to manage its identities ▪ Identities currently managed by a company do not need to be duplicated ▪ Identities extend down to the device/service and user level, not just the company level Scalable regarding many partners for data exchange ▪ Gradually expandable following to business needs and opportunities of a company MX-Port Leo: Think Big, Start Small, Scale Up!* 13 October 2025 3*) Asset Administration Shell – Quick Start Guide Step 3: standardized concepts for data sovereignty and trust Step 1: standardized common data models and data access Step 2: standardized discovery of the data provider 13 October 2025 4 MX-Port configuration “Leo” paves the way for companies to be able to share data with many partners in the future building on each other building on each other MX-Port configuration “Leo” MX-Port data provider MX-Port MX Gate MX Converter MX Adapter MX Gate MX Converter MX Adapter MX Discovery MX Acc. & Usg. NOAUTH F-X Trust List + URLs: Certificates of all F-X IdP(s) F-X MQTT Broker F-X MQTT Broker F-X MQTT Broker F-X token with Domain and Email/ServiceName IDTA Central F-X IDP Trusted public keys of F-X IDP(s) to check F-X token signature F-X Leo: Step3 (work in progress) Registry of Registries (slim services: serverList + assetFind) AAS Repository Yellow Pages Lookup service 1) Yellow Pages: Find company domain(s) with certain offer, e.g. motor supplier 2) Registry of Registries: Get server list for (each) company domain 3) Internal F-X IdP: provide company internal identity by X509 certificate chain or Entra ID and get F-X token with F-X identity: domain, email/serviceName, audience = provider 4) Make request with F-X token to Gateway which will forward request to servers, Gateway makes Token Exchange from F-X Token to provider internal access token, Client does not get provider internal access token 5) AAS Registry (for company domain): get AAS descriptor for Asset ID, through Gateway, AAS Security with Access Rules apply 6) AAS Repository (for company domain): get AAS(s) and Submodel(s) or Query, through Gateway, AAS Security with Access Rules apply 7) MQTT Broker (for company domain): Client must subscribe to all MQTT brokers through related Gateways, security applys, Messages from providers to consumers, Messages from consumers to providers, Topics /noauth, Demo Package Explorer IDTA Asset Data eXchange Hub 10 13.10.2025 Asset Data eXchange Hub Server Company A Data Provider Server Company B Server Company C Data Consumer Application A ERP Human Application B Maintenance Application C Engineering Application D PLM 11 Core Components 13.10.2025 Asset Data eXchange Hub: Core Components DIscovery SMT RepoIdentity Provider Validation Service Identity Provider • Central IDP for authentication and authorization. • Supports X.509 certificate-based authentication. • Integrates with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD). • Implements OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect protocols. Discovery • Discover business partners and their AAS endpoints • Integrated with the central IDP • Supports service-to-service interaction SMT Repo • Fetch IDTA submodel templates via APIs, available across all versions • Integrated with the central IDP. • Provides a UI with advanced search functionalities for Submodel templates. Validation Service • Supports AAS file validation and API validations. • Supports API validation for different API profiles based on IDTA specifications. • Provides support for service-to- service interaction. 12 Core Components 13.10.2025 Asset Data eXchange Hub: User Flow (Data Providers) AAS Server Company B AAS Server Company C AAS Server Company A 0.) Data providers identify & get authenticated → Added to trust list Data Provider Discovery SMT RepoIdentity Provider Validation Service 1313.10.2025 Asset Data eXchange Hub: User Flow (Data Providers) 1.) Data providers can obtain the latest version of the submodel templates via an API. AASPE to use multiple Repositories, Registries .. Clone: https://github.com/eclipse-aaspe/package-explorer.git 25 13/10/2025IDTA – The Standard for the Digital Twin (1) Use a Repo/ Reg. as starting point (2) A dynamic environment is built with retrieved AAS Identifiables (3) Editing is as normal (4) Every Identifiables manages its endpoint (5) Save of 'tainted' Identifiables is possible Retrieval: Principle of operation Get.. operation Edit Save 1 2 3 4 5 26 13.10.2025IDTA Introduction Demo: Retrieve AAS and browse Submodels 27 13.10.2025IDTA Introduction Demo: Retrieve different Identifiables and do paging 28 13.10.2025IDTA Introduction Demo: Retrieve Identifiables from a Query ➔ 29 13/10/2025IDTA Open Tech days 2025 ◼ User switches API interface set: Repo / Reg / Reg of Reg ◼ User manually selects which operation is to be used and provided parameters As of today: ◼ Query adapted to V3.1 standard ◼ RegOfReg to be adapted to last developments Retrieval (1) – Interface operations 30 13/10/2025IDTA Open Tech days 2025 ◼ Filter .. to filter GetAll.. elements ◼ Control auto-loading of dependent Identifiables ◼ Control parallel-fetches ◼ Control pagination ◼ Ids are typically encrypted by the UI (user enters in clear-text) Retrieval (2) – Quite some options 31 13/10/2025IDTA Introduction ◼ Each data space to align on the adequate security access modes ◼ Each AAS user application to be configured with the applicable configuration ◼ For the future, an interoperable configuration to be desirable Config of AASPE: AASPE implements all security access modes to meet the requirements of the data space Method Description Basic (Username/ Password) If deployability is key .. Certificate Store Certificates provided by the adminstrator of the IT Certificate File Certificates provided by users Interactive by browser e.g. ENTRA ID as with existing Microsoft accounts Secret based e.g OAuth2 Client Credential Flow 32 13.10.2025IDTA Introduction Demo: 5+1 different security access modes 33 13.10.2025IDTA Introduction Demo: 5+1 different security access modes 34 13/10/2025IDTA – The Standard for the Digital Twin ◼ Load local AASX ◼ Select AAS ◼ Trigger upload assistant Upload (1) 35 13/10/2025IDTA – The Standard for the Digital Twin ◼ UI need differentiate between POST or PUT ◼ User aware of versions to prevent overwrite of newest information Upload (2) 36 13/10/2025IDTA – The Standard for the Digital Twin Delete differentiates: ◼ local Identifiables ◼ remote Identifiables ◼ connected Submodels Delete .. and all the fiddly bits 1 2 3 4 5 13.10.2025IDTA Open Tech days 2025 Conclusions 38 13/10/2025IDTA Introduction MX Port Leo – Status Oct 2025 With M/X port Leo we're going quite far .. M/X port Leo world IDTA data space Data space e.g. Furniture-X ▪ AAS standards ✓ ▪ SMT repo ✓ ▪ AAS for everybody ✓ [..] Internet ▪ Identity providers ✓ ▪ Yellow pages ✓ ▪ Reg of Reg ✓ ▪ URL resolving ✓ ▪ Discovery services ✓ ▪ Registries & Repositories ✓ ▪ many AAS user applications ▪ ubiquitous data sources ▪ process integration ▪ AI and ontologies ▪ recurring innovation Catena-X with Data Space Connector with: ▪ secure communication ▪ using web standards ▪ sync & async access 39 13.10.2025IDTA Introduction AAS user applications Perceivable software stack AAS user applications ✓ AAS suite, bill-X, Mnestix, AAS.TwinEngine, Neoception DTI, twinsphere, AAS PE, Siemens tools, .. AAS client access layer ? instance includes type data, derivedFrom resolution, semanticId matching, iterator functions, caching of data, eventing, .. API operations ✓ Get all AAS, .. server frameworks ✓ aasx server, Basyx, FAST, .. persistency & data bases ✓ file spaces, Mongo, SQLite, .. multiple operating systems ✓ Windows, Linus, mac/OS, mobile? SMT specifications ✓ Digital nameplate, Technical data, .. AAS meta model ✓ UML model, serialzation, .. IEC & ISO standardization ✓ IEC 63278 series, IEC 61406, .. missing? 40 13.10.2025IDTA Introduction Instance includes type data • For asset instance, the data of the asset type is automatically joined Possible AAS client access layer Possible functional features, examples derivedFrom resolution • In value chains, Submodels and attributes might be automatically inherited from multiple AAS semanticId management • Equivalent information in different versions of a Submodel might be handled AAS asset instance SM Nameplate SM 3D model AAS asset type SM Tech data SM Handover doc.derivedFromderivedFrom AAS operator <I> AAS integrator <I> AAS supplier <I> AAS supplier <T> AAS family <T> ManufacturerProductRoot IEC CDD 0112/2///61360_7#AAS011#001 ManufacturerProductRoot ECLASS V.15 0173-1#02-AAU732#003 ManufacturerProductRoot ECLASS V.13 0173-1#02-AAU732#002 isEquivalentForThisSMT isEquivalentForThisSMT 41 13.10.2025IDTA Introduction Thank You for Your attention!
